expectations.text: Questions target specific gaps rather than restating the four stated requirements
expectations.evidence: Analysis identifies 'exactly four concrete claims' and explicitly focuses on what's missing. Questions target sandboxing (not mentioned by user), API contracts (not mentioned), failure handling (not mentioned), UI extension meaning (vague). No question restates the four claims.

expectations.text: Questions use concrete failure scenarios or edge cases rather than abstract inquiries
expectations.evidence: Q4: 'a third-party plugin throws an unhandled exception while rendering its UI component, or it makes a data source request that hangs indefinitely. Does the whole app crash? Does just the plugin's area go blank?' Q5: 'you ship a new version of your app that changes the internal structure — maybe you refactor how data flows.' Scenarios are vivid and specific.

expectations.text: Strategy explicitly plans to use AskUserQuestion one at a time
expectations.evidence: Every question states 'Would use AskUserQuestion: Yes' with notes about one-at-a-time delivery and how previous answers shape subsequent questions.

expectations.text: At least one question surfaces a non-obvious architectural concern (beyond obvious security/sandboxing)
expectations.evidence: Q5 about API stability/versioning surfaces the tension between moving fast on the host app and not breaking the ecosystem. Q3 about data sources probes plugin-to-plugin data sharing. Both go beyond the obvious security angle.

expectations.text: Strategy includes a mid-interview checkpoint or summary to redirect the conversation
expectations.evidence: Q6 is explicitly a checkpoint: 'Let me summarize where we are... Before we go further — I still want to explore how plugins are distributed... Are those the right areas to focus on, or is there something else on your mind?' Directly follows the skill's interview rhythm guidance.
